title: pi-astack settings
description: Configuration schema for pi-astack extensions. Lives at ~/.pi/agent/pi-astack-settings.json. Each top-level key namespaces one extension; missing sections fall back to that extension's hardcoded DEFAULTS. To enable IDE autocomplete + validation, add "$schema": "./path/to/pi-astack-settings.schema.json" as the first field in your settings file.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema file. Used by IDEs for validation and autocomplete; ignored at runtime.

_comment.description: Free-form comment field. JSON has no native comments; use this (or any underscore-prefixed key) for inline documentation.

modelCurator.description: Whitelist + capability hints for chat models. The curator filters pi-ai's hundreds of built-in model definitions to a curated keep-list per provider, then injects a markdown capability table into the main session system prompt on every turn.

providers.description: Keep-list per provider. Keys are provider names (anthropic, openai, deepseek, ...); values are arrays of model IDs to retain. All other models in that provider are removed from the registry. Providers not listed here are left untouched.

additionalProperties.description: Model IDs to keep for this provider

hints.description: One-line capability hints shown next to each chat model in the system prompt table. Keys are 'provider/modelId'. Style: present tense, max ~15 words, name a concrete strength so the LLM can reason about role fit.

examples.anthropic/claude-opus-4-7: Strongest reasoning; security audits, architecture critique. Highest cost.
examples.openai/gpt-5.5: Strong general-purpose reasoning + coding. Image input.

imageGen.description: Image generation models. NOT registered as pi Models (they can't do chat) — listed in a separate 'Image generation' section of the system prompt as informational hints only. Keys are model IDs (no provider prefix).

examples.gpt-image-2: Latest OpenAI image generation model. Call `imagine(prompt: "...", size?, quality?, style?)` to generate images.

modelFallback.description: Multi-model fallback chain with retry (extensions/model-fallback/). Asymmetric policy: the **initial** model gets pi's full retry budget (default 3 retries = 4 attempts; for claude-code parity 9 retries = 10 attempts) before fallback kicks in; **each fallback model** gets exactly 1 attempt — any error switches to the next configured model immediately. When all fallback models are tried and all fail, the extension stops.

fallbackModels.description: Ordered list of `provider/modelId` strings. When the initial model exhausts its retries, the extension walks this list in order, picking the first un-tried entry that exists in pi's modelRegistry and has configured auth. Entries missing from the registry or without auth are logged as warnings and skipped. The currently-active model (whatever it is when the error happens) is auto-added to the tried set, so it's safe to include it in the list. Empty list = retry behavior only, no fallback.

initialRetries.description: How many retries pi will attempt **on the initial model** before model-fallback switches to the first fallback model. **Must match pi's settings.json#retry.maxRetries** — the extension's give-up node aligns to pi's, and a mismatch will either skip fallback (if this is too low and pi keeps retrying) or fire fallback before pi's retries are done (if this is too high; pi already gave up but extension is still waiting). Recommended claude-code-parity values: pi.retry.maxRetries=9, pi.retry.baseDelayMs=1000, initialRetries=9 → 10 total attempts on initial model with delays 1s,2s,4s,8s,16s,32s,64s,128s,256s (worst case ~8.5min per turn). Fallback models always get exactly 1 attempt regardless of this setting — any error on a fallback switches immediately to the next one.

vision.description: Vision tool model selection (extensions/vision/). Auto-selects the strongest vision-capable model from the registry, excluding the caller's own model.

modelPreferences.description: Ordered preference list, most-preferred first. Each entry is 'provider/idSubstring' — substring match on model id (case-insensitive, version-suffix-tolerant). Models matching earlier entries win; unmatched models still participate, ordered by cost.input descending as a rough capability proxy.
